summary: Reis argues that getting buy-in for data modeling requires articulating value in terms stakeholders understand, showing what's in it for them rather than speaking in technical jargon. He emphasizes that the current AI hype cycle presents a strategic opportunity to finally get organizational support for data housekeeping and modeling initiatives that have long been neglected.
key_points: Articulate value in terms of ROI and benefits to others, not from a place of selfishness
Most people don't care about data modeling - speak their language and avoid technical jargon
Use the AI hype cycle strategically to get buy-in for data foundations
Different organizations have vastly different attitudes toward data modeling maturity
Meet people where they are with simple diagrams showing how data relates to business processes
money_quotes: Too often, people try to get buy-in from others from a place of selfishness and lack of empathy. This is almost always a mistake.
Don't use terms like 'normalization' or 'surrogate key' to people who won't know what these mean.
With seemingly every board and CEO fixated on how their company can become AI-driven, educate stakeholders on why good data means AI that serves the business.
If there's ever a time to move on building your data and AI foundation, now is the time (especially before the hype cycle bursts).
Data isn't top of mind for most people, even though organizations are supposedly 'data driven' these days.
key_insight: The AI hype cycle is a rare window of opportunity to secure organizational buy-in for foundational data work like modeling—seize it before the bubble bursts.
